memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Pedigree of the family of Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) in Collections Historical & Archaeological Relating to Montgomeryshire, Vol. V, pages 158-159

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer)
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer). The record skips to the son of Henry, Herbert FitzHenry (Herbert FitzHerbert I, and his son Herbert FitzHerbert (II).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Vivonne於大約1251年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特加里 馬利特時，她的父親William de Forz31歲，母親Maud de Ferrers23歲。她於1262年3月10日之後與Sir Ingram de Percy在英國英格蘭結婚。她去世於1314年6月1日在英國薩默塞特修頓 門迪普享年64歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Vivonne was born about 1251, in Curry Mallet, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, William de Forz, was 31 and her mother, Maud de Ferrers, was 23. She married Sir Ingram de Percy after 10 March 1262, in England, United Kingdom. She died on 1 June 1314, in Chewton Mendip, Somerset, England, at the age of 64.

Elle a épousé Sir Ingram de Percy après 10 mars 1262, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1314, à Chewton Mendip, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Vivonne(이)가 대략 1251년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 커리 맬렛에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Forz(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Ferrers(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1262년 3월 10일 이후 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Ingram de Percy(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1314년 6월 1일 영국 서머세트 체w턴 멘디프에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
